Fa.rn Porrrrcar PnacrrcBs C ourrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . ,128 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
February 28,2001
Alan R. Bums, City Attomey
City of Fountain Valley
City Hall
10200 Slater Avenue
Fountain Valley, Califonria 92708
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-026
Dear Mr. Burns:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of City Councilman
John Collins regarding the gift reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1
QUESTION
If Councilman Collins accepted from a city vendor a ticket to attend a charitable
organization's fundraiser at which a meal was served, does Regulation 18946.4(b), which
states that tickets to such fundraising events have no value, apply?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Because the organization hosting the fundraiser is a nonprofit organization
exempt from taxation under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code, pursuant to
Regulation 18946.4(b), the ticket has no value and Councilman Collins did not receive a
reportable gift.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appeu at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
qil?


File No. A-01-026
Page No. 2
FACTS
John Collins, a Fountain Valley Council member, attended a fundraiser for the
Boys and Girls Club, which is exempt from taxation under Intemal Revenue Code $
501(c)(3). The tickets to the fundraiser were $50 each. As a part of the fundraiser, each
participant received a meal. The ticket to the fundraiser was purchased for Councilman
Collins by Rainbow Disposal, the City's franchise fiash hauler.
ANALYSIS
Regulation 18946.4 deals with the valuation and reporting of tickets to fundraisers
as gifts. Regulation 18946.40) specifically addresses fundraising events for certain
nonprofit organizations and states that:
"Where the event is a fundraising event for an organization exempt
from ta:<ation under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue
Code, the ticket or other admission privilege has no value."
The Boys and Girls Club is a nonprofit organization exempt from taxation under
Section 501(c)(3) of the Intemal Revenue Code. Pursuant to the above regulation, the
ticket for the Boys and Girls Club fundraiser had no value for either disclosure or
disqualification purposes. Therefore, the ticket that Councilman Collins received from
Rainbow Disposal has no value and does not constitute a reportable gift. (Whtte Advice
Letter, No. A-97-056)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
IIABjg
